title: prunus divaricata subsp. ursina
intro: Prunus divaricata subsp. ursina, also known as the Japanese plum or Japanese apricot, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Prunus divaricata subsp. ursina include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
